name: Freshping Uptime Monitoring API
what_it_does: Uptime monitoring service from Freshworks with REST API for managing checks, retrieving availability data, and managing status pages — monitoring websites, APIs, and ports from 10 global locations with alerting integration.
use_cases: Querying uptime check results and availability metrics for SLA reporting agents
Creating and managing website monitoring checks programmatically via API
Retrieving incident history for postmortem automation workflows
Integrating Freshping data with Freshdesk for automated ticket creation on downtime
Monitoring API endpoint availability from multiple global locations
not_for: Complex synthetic transaction monitoring (use Datadog Synthetics or New Relic Synthetics)
Real user monitoring and browser performance (use Datadog RUM or New Relic Browser)
best_when: A Freshworks ecosystem team needs simple uptime monitoring with API access and status pages, integrated with Freshdesk for ticket creation on downtime.
avoid_when: You need complex synthetic monitoring, browser testing, or monitoring outside the Freshworks ecosystem.

auth.notes: API key from Freshping account. Passed as Authorization header. Account-scoped access. Freshworks SSO integration for enterprise accounts. Token management in account settings.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $13/month; Growth from $53; Pro from $118
pricing.notes: Generous free tier — 50 checks is substantial for small teams. Part of Freshworks suite. Good value when already using Freshdesk or other Freshworks products. 10 global monitoring locations.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers checks, reports, and status pages. Webhook support for downtime alerts. API documentation limited compared to some competitors. Freshworks ecosystem integration adds value.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All monitoring data queries are idempotent reads. Check creation/update operations are idempotent. Status page management operations are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation not as comprehensive as standalone monitoring tools
Deep value requires Freshworks ecosystem — standalone use is functional but limited
Rate limits not publicly documented — test before production use
Status page API separate from monitoring API — different endpoint patterns

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. SOC2 Type II certified (Freshworks). GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. Enterprise-grade security from established public company.
